trivia_bullets.text: Dragon Trainer is a generic, unnamed Human from Magic: The Gathering Foundations (FDN, November 2024). The Foundations set was designed as a long-print-run entry-point set with a five-year Standard legality window (2024-2029), replacing the Core Set format. The card's type line is simply 'Creature — Human'; the flavor text implies a lifelong bond with the dragon but names no character. [Scryfall]

Flavor text: "I was hardly a month old when he hatched. We grew up together. Even shared a crib!" [Scryfall]. The first-person voice implies the trainer raised the dragon from hatching — framing the bond as familial rather than professional.

Mana cost {3}{R}{R}, a five-mana red creature with 1/1 body; on entry creates a 4/4 red Dragon token with flying — the token doing the combat work the trainer's stats cannot. EDHREC rank 16564. Legal in Standard, Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, Vintage, and Commander. [Scryfall] [WebSearch: TCGPlayer Dragon Trainer Foundations]

Art by Steve Prescott, who has illustrated 234 MTG cards since 2006, with work spanning core sets, commander products, and multiple expansions. Prescott's background includes Dungeons & Dragons interior art, Paizo Pathfinder, and World of Warcraft TCG. [Wikipedia: Steve Prescott (artist)] [Gatherer: artist search Steve Prescott]

The 4/4 red Dragon creature token generated by Dragon Trainer is illustrated by Alexander Ostrowski on the Foundations Tokens sheet (TFDN no. 16) — a distinct commission from the main card art. [Scryfall: TFDN 16 via WebSearch snippet]
